[[BLOCK:slide10_shape1]]
R-squared (R平方 (R Square)) is 0.199. Not too bad! This model can explain 20% of the variance in weight.  
However, when multiple predictors are used, the program applies the adjusted R-squared to correct for the inflated R-squared.  
The result is a negative value! Since R-squared is r * r, it theoretically should not be negative. What is going on here?
